aggregate_brief: Today's standouts: GB Power — 91th-percentile of 5-yr range — historically high; EU Storage — 14.0 pp below the 5-yr seasonal average.

news.geopolitics_summary: EU ETS review due July; Poland-led climate backlash; energy security dominates Commission agenda amid fossil fuel weaponization warnings.

themes.headline: EU ETS review scheduled July; Poland signals political resistance to carbon policy
themes.why_it_matters: EUA price at risk if review signals weakening ambition or widening exemptions. Poland's vocal criticism signals Member State coalition risk; July review could trigger volatility in EUA curve (front-month and Cal+1).

themes.headline: Energy Commissioner demands Big Tech data centers transition to carbon-free power
themes.why_it_matters: Regulatory push for carbon-free baseload from hyperscalers drives long-term demand for dispatchable renewables and storage. Signals structural power demand growth; sustains higher DA and Cal+1 spreads via must-run renewable curtailment risk.

themes.headline: EU chiefs warn fossil fuel reliance poses defense and geopolitical weaponization risk
themes.why_it_matters: Reinvigorates EU strategic autonomy narrative, supporting accelerated renewables and gas infrastructure diversification (LNG, interconnects). Backstops long-term carbon ambition and power-curve risk premia; TTF winter curve may reflect higher investment in alternative baseload.

themes.headline: US natural gas for power flat summer 2026; record highs forecast summer 2027
themes.why_it_matters: Signals sustained US gas burn despite mild summer demand. Indirectly affects European LNG arb: if US gen demand stays high, fewer cargoes available for export, tightening TTF via reduced LNG supply elasticity into winter 2026–27.

themes.headline: 44.9 Bcf/d US natural gas pipeline capacity additions planned 2026–2027, Texas-led
themes.why_it_matters: Rising US export infrastructure capacity (Permian takeaway, LNG feed) expands potential global LNG supply. If capacity reaches terminals, increases LNG availability to Europe, pressuring TTF spot and Cal+1 via improved US–NW Europe arbitrage at higher export levels.

watchlist: EU ETS review by Commission, July 2026: EUA curve volatility risk.
POLITICO Energy and Climate Forum outcomes: energy security and climate policy momentum signals.
Turkey COP31 hosting June 2026: Cyprus exclusion row; may delay or muddy carbon policy consensus.
US LNG terminal approvals and export ramp 2026–2027: monitor TTF arb compression.
